title: SDAIA AI Ethics Principles
citation: SDAIA, AI Ethics Principles, version 2, September 2023
summary: SDAIA's national framework for ethical AI across the system lifecycle. It sets seven core principles, a four tier risk classification for AI systems, and roles and responsibilities for developers and deployers, with SDAIA as the supervising authority.
impact_note: Although non binding, this is the reference point Saudi regulators and government buyers use when assessing AI systems, and it aligns deliberately with the PDPL on personal data questions. Organisations developing or deploying AI in the Kingdom should map their systems to the four risk tiers, document conformity with the seven principles, and expect the framework's expectations to surface in procurement requirements and any future binding AI rules.
notes: Version 2 issued September 2023; the exact publication day needs confirmation and the source link should be replaced with the SDAIA document. Version 1 of September 2022 should be added as a separate record with a supersedes relationship from this one.
